rationale.root_cause: At the FLAW site in CWE122_Heap_Based_Buffer_Overflow__char_type_overrun_memcpy_01_bad (around line 42), memcpy uses sizeof(*structCharVoid) to copy into charFirst[16], overrunning the 16-byte array and overwriting voidSecond. This is the concrete heap buffer overflow mechanism corrupting the pointer field.
rationale.local_fix: Change the memcpy length from sizeof(*structCharVoid) to sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst) so only the character field is copied. This prevents overwriting the subsequent pointer fields.
rationale.global_fix: Introduce and require a bounded field-copy helper or a SIZEOF_FIELD(type, field) macro so copy sizes are always derived from the destination field, not the struct. This prevents confusion between struct size and field size across the codebase.
